mission.title: cert-manager: Use multivalue records instead of simple records
mission.description:
Use multivalue records instead of simple records for the AWS Route53 ACME DNS challenge solver.
When using simple records, it would be impossible to create a second record in a Route53 hosted zone with the same name/type.

This way when either using the same domain name on multiple clusters or using the same CNAME record for multiple domains would create a new challenge instead of overwriting the other challenges.

### Pull Request Motivation
Fix https://github.com/jetstack/cert-manager/issues/3460

### Kind
bug

### Release Note

```release-note
Use multivalue records instead of simple records for the AWS Route53 ACME DNS challenge solver, to allow for multiple challenges for the same domain at the same time
```

resolution.summary:
### Pull Request Motivation
There is a race condition where multiple cert-managers in different clusters try to validate the ownership of a same domain using DNS01 challenge, when issuing certificates that have the same commonName or overlapping dnsNames.

For instance, imaging below certificates are simultaneously submitted to its corresponding cluster:
Cert applied to cluster-1
```yaml
apiVersion: cert-manager.io/v1
kind: Certificate
metadata:
  name: cert-for-cluster-1
spec:
  commonName: app.cluster-1.example.com
  dnsNames:
    - app.example.com
  issuerRef:
    kind: ClusterIssuer
    name: letsencrypt
```
Cert applied to cluster-2
```yaml
apiVersion: cert-manager.io/v1
kind: Certificate
metadata:
  name: cert-for-cluster-2
spec:
  commonName: app.cluster-2.example.com
  dnsNames:
    - app.example.com
  issuerRef:
    kind: ClusterIssuer
    name: letsencrypt
```
When using DNS01 challenge to validate the ownership of `app.example.com`, Let's Encrypt will handout different tokens for each cluster/cert-manager. The happy path would be: `UPDATE_CNAME_FOR_CLUSTER1 -> VALIDATED_IN_CLUSTER1 ->  UPDATE_CNAME_FOR_CLUSTER2 -> VALIDATED_IN_CLUSTER2`. The race condition, however, happens when the CNAME record value got updated before cert-manager (let's encrypt) in the first cluster finishing validating. In this case, the challenge for `app.example.com` will stuck in `pending` state in cluster-1 forever, result in certs won't be issued. Currently, someone need to manually delete
steps: Review the issue discussion for context
Apply the fix from the linked pull request
codeSnippets:
### Pull Request Motivation
There is a race condition where multiple cert-managers in different clusters try to validate the ownership of a same domain using DNS01 challenge, when issuing certificates that have the same commonName or overlapping dnsNames.

For instance, imaging below certificates are simultaneously submitted to its corresponding cluster:
Cert applied to cluster-1

When using DNS01 challenge to validate the ownership of `app.example.com`, Let's Encrypt will handout different tokens for each cluster/cert-manager. The happy path would be: `UPDATE_CNAME_FOR_CLUSTER1 -> VALIDATED_IN_CLUSTER1 ->  UPDATE_CNAME_FOR_CLUSTER2 -> VALIDATED_IN_CLUSTER2`. The race condition, however, happens when the CNAME record value got updated before cert-manager (let's encrypt) in the first cluster finishing validating. In this case, the challenge for `app.example.com` will stuck in `pending` state in cluster-1 forever, result in certs won't be issued. Currently, someone need to manually delete the stuck challenge so cert-manager can update the DNS record again to complete the challenge validation.

Therefore, we would like to add a new feature so the system can self-heal and become eventually consistent on its own. Besides, it can also be used to solve other general stuck challenge issue.

#### Feature Description

- A new argument `dns01-check-recreate-period` is added to allow setting a deadline before the _pending_ challenge gets deleted and recreated.
- The default value is 0 which indicates never auto-delete any pending challenge, so this feature is fully backward compatible
- If DNS01 check recreation is enabled and the pending challenge's creation time passed the deadline, the syncer will delete the challenge automatically. The challenge will be created by other parts of cert-manager so DNS record for the challege can be update the proper value hence complete the validation.

### Kind
feature

### Release Note
